title: The One Where Everybody Finds Out

jokes.text: Ironically, most of the boxes seem to be labeled 'Clothes.'
jokes.setup: Ugly Naked Guy is moving and packing boxes
jokes.punchline: The naked guy owns clothes, creating ironic contrast
jokes.explanation: Clean observational humor with good ironic contrast. Well-structured with economy of language.

jokes.text: Aw, I'm going to miss that big old squishy butt. And we're done with the chicken fried rice.
jokes.setup: Ross sentimentally talking about missing something
jokes.punchline: Juxtaposition of naked guy's butt with food creates gross-out humor
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and timing. The quick pivot from sentiment to disgust is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Yeah, hey, then we could do that telephone thing... you know, where you have a can, we have a can... and it's connected by a string. Or we could do the actual telephone thing.
jokes.setup: Joey suggesting ways to communicate across apartments
jokes.punchline: His elaborate description of a primitive communication method when phones exist
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character moment - his childlike enthusiasm for overcomplicated solutions.

jokes.text: That is because your eye immediately goes to the big naked man.
jokes.setup: Rachel commenting she never noticed how nice the apartment was
jokes.punchline: Ross explains the obvious distraction
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that explains the setup logically. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Aah! Aah! Aah! Chandler and Monica. Chandler and Monica. Oh, my God. Oh, my eyes! My eyes!
jokes.setup: Characters looking across at the apartment
jokes.punchline: Horrified reaction to seeing Monica and Chandler having sex
jokes.explanation: Major plot revelation delivered through physical comedy. High impact moment that drives the episode.

jokes.text: Nothing. Oh, God, we're just so excited that you might get this apartment.
jokes.setup: Ross asking what's wrong after the screaming
jokes.punchline: Rachel's obviously fake excuse while still traumatized
jokes.explanation: Good cover-up humor that maintains the awkward energy of the discovery.

jokes.text: Uh-huh. Doing it, doing it, phone doing it.
jokes.setup: Phoebe listing Monica and Chandler's supposed activities
jokes.punchline: Rachel's rhythmic confirmation with 'phone doing it' as escalation
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with good rhythm and escalation. Very quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: I mean, I think it's great. For him. She might be able to do better.
jokes.setup: Phoebe commenting on Monica and Chandler's relationship
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment suggesting Chandler is lucky
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe delivery - seemingly supportive but actually insulting Chandler.

jokes.text: You mean how they're friends and nothing more?
jokes.setup: Being told Phoebe knows about Monica and Chandler
jokes.punchline: Joey's attempt to maintain the secret through obvious sarcasm
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Joey's poor lying skills in a panic.

jokes.text: Actually, we saw them doing it up against the window.
jokes.setup: Explaining they saw Monica and Chandler through the window
jokes.punchline: Graphic detail that makes the situation more awkward
jokes.explanation: Adds cringe factor through uncomfortable detail. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Or we could not tell them we know and have a little fun of our own.
jokes.setup: Rachel wanting to end the secrecy
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's mischievous alternative plan
jokes.explanation: Sets up the main plot of the episode. Perfect Phoebe mischief with good story structure.

jokes.text: You know, every time they say they're doing laundry... we'll give them laundry to do.
jokes.setup: Explaining how to mess with Monica and Chandler
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of their cover story
jokes.explanation: Brilliant comedic concept - turning their lie against them. Great structure and originality.

jokes.text: You don't have any secrets. Oh, yeah? Well, you don't know about Hugsy, my bedtime penguin pal.
jokes.setup: Joey claiming to have secrets of his own
jokes.punchline: His 'secret' is an embarrassing stuffed animal
jokes.explanation: Iconic character reveal that became a running gag. Perfect Joey vulnerability moment.

jokes.text: My sexuality.
jokes.setup: Phoebe describing her strongest tool for messing with them
jokes.punchline: Deadpan delivery of obvious weapon
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing. Phoebe's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Watch, learn, and don't eat my cookie.
jokes.setup: Phoebe about to demonstrate her plan
jokes.punchline: Adding the random cookie concern
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phoebe's priorities and random concerns.

jokes.text: You've been working out? Well, I try to, you know, squeeze things.
jokes.setup: Phoebe flirting by asking about working out
jokes.punchline: Chandler's awkward response about squeezing things
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Chandler's discomfort with the unexpected flirting.

jokes.text: Well, actually, you're the one person I can't tell this to. And the one person I want to the most.
jokes.setup: Phoebe pretending to have feelings she can't share
jokes.punchline: The ironic twist that he's both forbidden and desired
jokes.explanation: Well-crafted fake seduction with good dramatic irony since audience knows the truth.

jokes.text: Oh, no. Have I said too much?
jokes.setup: Phoebe's fake confession building
jokes.punchline: Fake concern about revealing too much
jokes.explanation: Solid continuation of the seduction bit, maintaining the awkward energy.

jokes.text: Well, my grandfather was Swedish. And my grandmother was actually a tiny little bunny.
jokes.setup: Monica asking how Chandler got to be so cute
jokes.punchline: Chandler's ridiculous genealogy involving a bunny
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler absurdist humor. Creative and unexpected while staying in character.

jokes.text: I think Phoebe was hitting on me. What are you talking about? I'm telling you, I think Phoebe thinks I'm foxy.
jokes.setup: Chandler reporting Phoebe's behavior
jokes.punchline: His confident use of 'foxy' while Monica dismisses him
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's ego boost and Monica's skepticism.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, it's just Phoebe's just always thought you were charming... in a sexless kind of way.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to let Chandler down easy
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about his charm being non-sexual
jokes.explanation: Perfectly cutting insult disguised as reassurance. Great character dynamic.

jokes.text: Oh, well, you know, I can't hear that enough.
jokes.setup: Monica's 'sexless' comment
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic response to the insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler sarcasm in response to being insulted by his girlfriend.

jokes.text: This bicep? Well, it's not flexed right now.
jokes.setup: Monica questioning which bicep Phoebe touched
jokes.punchline: Chandler's defensive excuse about his muscle not being flexed
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment showing Chandler's insecurity about his physique.

jokes.text: [Rachel dumps a huge bag of laundry] You don't mind, do you? That would really help me out a lot. Thanks.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler saying they're going to do laundry
jokes.punchline: Rachel literally giving them a huge amount of laundry to do
jokes.explanation: Perfect execution of Phoebe's plan. Great visual gag and callback with excellent timing.

jokes.text: A basket of mini muffins. There's a whole table of mini muffin baskets. Which one did you send? The small one.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining his bribe to get the apartment
jokes.punchline: The revelation that his small basket is competing with many others
jokes.explanation: Great escalating reveal showing Ross's naivety. Good structure building to the smallest basket punchline.

jokes.text: Someone sent us a basket at work once and people went crazy over those muffins. It was the best day. Your work makes me sad.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining why he thought muffins would work
jokes.punchline: Joey's brutal assessment of Ross's workplace
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey deadpan response. Great economy and character insight about Ross's boring museum job.

jokes.text: Bye, Chandler. I miss you already.
jokes.setup: Group leaving for a movie
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's overly intimate goodbye to Chandler
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Phoebe's fake seduction plan, maintaining the awkwardness.

jokes.text: Okay, but what about my pinchable butt and my bulging biceps?
jokes.setup: Monica explaining Phoebe knows about them
jokes.punchline: Chandler's ego-driven concern about his attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's vanity despite understanding the situation.

jokes.text: But they don't know that we know that they know.
jokes.setup: Discussing the complex knowledge situation
jokes.punchline: The tongue-twisting logic of who knows what
jokes.explanation: Iconic line that became a meme. Great wordplay and perfectly captures the absurd situation.

jokes.text: The messers become the messees.
jokes.setup: Planning to mess with Phoebe and Rachel back
jokes.punchline: Chandler's made-up word construction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler wordplay. His love of made-up words and awkward constructions.

jokes.text: Like if I wanted something from Joey... I would strike up a conversation about, say, um, sandwiches... or, uh, my underwear.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining how to bond with someone using their interests
jokes.punchline: Joey's immediate interest when underwear is mentioned
jokes.explanation: Perfect character insight into Joey's simple interests, with great reaction beat.

jokes.text: So he likes to break stuff.
jokes.setup: Listing things Ugly Naked Guy broke
jokes.punchline: Rachel's logical but absurd conclusion
jokes.explanation: Good logical leap humor, though not particularly memorable or character-specific.

jokes.text: Poor cat never saw that big butt coming.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Ugly Naked Guy's former cat
jokes.punchline: Implication the cat died from being sat on
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good callback structure. Memorable and quotable line.

jokes.text: Why don't you come over and I'll let you, uh, feel my bicep. Or maybe more.
jokes.setup: Chandler calling Phoebe to escalate the fake flirtation
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt at seduction
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Chandler out of his element trying to be seductive.

jokes.text: They don't know that we know they know we know.
jokes.setup: The knowledge situation getting more complex
jokes.punchline: Even more complicated version of the 'know' tongue twister
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the earlier joke. Even more quotable and became iconic.

jokes.text: Couldn't if I wanted to.
jokes.setup: Being told not to say anything about the complex situation
jokes.punchline: Joey admitting he doesn't understand it anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - admitting confusion with great economy and character truth.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, I can't help but notice that you're naked and... I applaud you. Man, I wish I was naked.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to bond with Ugly Naked Guy
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt to relate to nudity
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Ross's desperation and social awkwardness.

jokes.text: That is how God intended it.
jokes.setup: Ross continuing to bond with the naked guy
jokes.punchline: Philosophical justification for nudity
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Ross's awkward bonding attempt with pseudo-philosophical twist.
